de.summary: ST-GDance++ erzeugt koordinierte Gruppentanzbewegungen aus Musik, indem räumlich-zeitliche Abhängigkeiten durch leichte Graphfaltungen und effizientes Aufmerksamkeits-Masking entkoppelt werden, wodurch skalierbare Choreographie mit reduzierter Latenz für Film- und Gaming-Anwendungen ermöglicht wird.

es.title: ST-GDance++: Una Difusión Espacial-Temporal Escalable para Coreografía de Grupo de Larga Duración
es.summary: ST-GDance++ genera movimientos de danza grupal coordinados a partir de la música desacoplando dependencias espacial-temporales con convoluciones gráficas ligeras y enmascaramiento de atención eficiente, permitiendo coreografía escalable con latencia reducida para aplicaciones de cine y videojuegos.

title: Geometric Mixture-of-Experts with Curvature-Guided Adaptive Routing for Graph Representation Learning
summary: GeoMoE framework adaptively routes node representations across diverse Riemannian spaces using Ollivier-Ricci curvature as geometric guidance, better capturing topological heterogeneity in graph-structured data.
content:
arXiv:2603.22317v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Graph-structured data typically exhibits complex topological heterogeneity, making it difficult to model accurately within a single Riemannian manifold. While emerging mixed-curvature methods attempt to capture such diversity, they often rely on implicit, task-driven routing that lacks fundamental geometric grounding. To address this challenge, we propose a Geometric Mixture-of-Experts framework (GeoMoE) that adaptively fuses node representations across diverse Riemannian spaces to better accommodate multi-scale topological structures. At its core, GeoMoE leverages Ollivier-Ricci Curvature (ORC) as an intrinsic geometric prior to orchestrate the collaboration of specialized experts. Specifically, we design a graph-aware gating network that assigns node-specific fusion weights, regularized by a curvature-guided alignment loss to ensure interpretable and geometry-consistent routing. Additionally, we introduce a curvature-aware contrastive objective that promotes geometric discriminability by constructing positive and negative pairs according to curvature consistency. Extensive experiments on six benchmark datasets demonstrate that GeoMoE outperforms state-of-the-art baselines across diverse graph types.

zh.title: 几何混合专家与曲率引导自适应路由的图表示学习
zh.summary: GeoMoE框架使用Ollivier-Ricci曲率作为几何指导，自适应地在不同的黎曼空间中路由节点表示，更好地捕捉图结构数据中的拓扑异质性。

fr.title: Mélange Géométrique d'Experts avec Routage Adaptatif Guidé par la Courbure pour l'Apprentissage de Représentation de Graphes
fr.summary: Le framework GeoMoE achemine de manière adaptative les représentations de nœuds dans divers espaces riemanniens en utilisant la courbure d'Ollivier-Ricci comme guidance géométrique, capturant mieux l'hétérogénéité topologique dans les données structurées en graphes.

de.title: Geometrische Mixture-of-Experts mit Krümmungs-geführetes adaptives Routing für Graph-Repräsentationslernlen
de.summary: Das GeoMoE-Framework leitet Knotendarstellungen adaptiv in verschiedene Riemannsche Räume ein und nutzt die Ollivier-Ricci-Krümmung als geometrische Anleitung, um die topologische Heterogenität in graphstrukturierten Daten besser zu erfassen.

es.title: Mezcla Geométrica de Expertos con Enrutamiento Adaptativo Guiado por Curvatura para Aprendizaje de Representación de Grafos
es.summary: El framework GeoMoE enruta de manera adaptativa las representaciones de nodos en espacios de Riemann diversos utilizando la curvatura de Ollivier-Ricci como guía geométrica, capturando mejor la heterogeneidad topológica en datos estructurados en grafos.

title: Sparsely-Supervised Data Assimilation via Physics-Informed Schr\"odinger Bridge
summary: PICSB framework enables fast spatiotemporal field reconstruction from sparse observations by conditioning generative models on low-fidelity priors while incorporating PDE constraints, eliminating need for full-field supervision during training.
content:
arXiv:2603.22319v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Data assimilation (DA) for systems governed by partial differential equations (PDE) aims to reconstruct full spatiotemporal fields from sparse high-fidelity (HF) observations while respecting physical constraints. While full-grid low-fidelity (LF) simulations provide informative priors in multi-fidelity settings, recovering an HF field consistent with both sparse observations and the governing PDE typically requires per-instance test-time optimization, which becomes a major bottleneck in time-critical applications. To alleviate this, amortized reconstruction using generative models has recently been proposed; however, such approaches rely on full-field HF supervision during training, which is often impractical in real-world settings. From a more realistic perspective, we propose the Physics-Informed Conditional Schr\"odinger Bridge (PICSB), which transports an informative LF prior toward an observation-conditioned HF posterior without any additional inference-time guidance. To enable learning without HF endpoints, PICSB employs an iterative surrogate-endpoint refresh scheme, and directly incorporates PDE residuals into the training objective while enforcing observations via hard conditioning throughout sampling. Experiments on fluid PDE benchmarks demonstrate that PICSB enables extremely fast spatiotemporal field reconstruction while maintaining competitive accuracy under sparse HF supervision.

zh.title: 稀疏监督数据同化通过物理信息薛定谔桥
zh.summary: PICSB框架通过对低保真先验进行条件化生成模型并纳入PDE约束，从稀疏观测中实现快速时空场重建，消除了训练期间对全场监督的需求。

fr.title: Assimilation de Données Faiblement Supervisées via le Pont de Schrödinger Informé par la Physique
fr.summary: Le cadre PICSB permet une reconstruction rapide de champs spatio-temporels à partir d'observations clairsemées en conditionnant les modèles génératifs sur des priors de basse fidélité tout en incorporant des contraintes EDP, éliminant le besoin de supervision du champ complet lors de l'entraînement.

de.title: Dünn Überwachte Datenassimilation durch Physik-informierte Schrödinger-Brücke
de.summary: Das PICSB-Framework ermöglicht schnelle raum-zeitliche Feldrekonstruktion aus spärlichen Beobachtungen durch Konditionierung generativer Modelle auf niedrig-fidelitätspriors bei Einbeziehung von PDE-Nebenbedingungen, was die Notwendigkeit vollständiger Feldüberwachung während des Trainings beseitigt.

es.title: Asimilación de Datos Débilmente Supervisada mediante Puente de Schrödinger Informado por Física
es.summary: El marco PICSB permite la reconstrucción rápida de campos espacio-temporales a partir de observaciones dispersas condicionando modelos generativos en priors de baja fidelidad mientras se incorporan restricciones de EDP, eliminando la necesidad de supervisión de campo completo durante el entrenamiento.

title: From Instructions to Assistance: a Dataset Aligning Instruction Manuals with Assembly Videos for Evaluating Multimodal LLMs
summary: M2AD dataset evaluates multimodal LLMs on furniture assembly tasks by aligning instruction manuals with video, revealing performance limitations in multi-image reasoning and architectural constraints for real-time technical assistance.
content:
arXiv:2603.22321v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: The recent advancements introduced by Large Language Models (LLMs) have transformed how Artificial Intelligence (AI) can support complex, real world tasks, pushing research outside the text boundaries towards multi modal contexts and leading to Multimodal Large Language Models (MLMs). Given the current adoption of LLM based assistants in solving technical or domain specific problems, the natural continuation of this trend is to extend the input domains of these assistants exploiting MLMs. Ideally, these MLMs should be used as real time assistants in procedural tasks, hopefully integrating a view of the environment where the user being assisted is, or even better sharing the same point of view via Virtual Reality (VR) or Augmented Reality (AR) supports, to reason over the same scenario the user is experiencing. With this work, we aim at evaluating the quality of currently openly available MLMs to provide this kind of assistance on technical tasks. To this end, we annotated a data set of furniture assembly with step by step labels and manual references: the Manual to Action Dataset (M2AD). We used this dataset to assess (1) to which extent the reasoning abilities of MLMs can be used to reduce the need for detailed labelling, allowing for more efficient, cost effective annotation practices, (2) whether MLMs are able to track the progression of assembly steps (3) and whether MLMs can refer correctly to the instruction manual pages. Our results showed that while some models understand procedural sequences, their performance is limited by architectural and hardware constraints, highlighting the need for multi image and interleaved text image reasoning.

zh.title: 从指令到协助：对齐说明书与装配视频以评估多模态大语言模型的数据集
zh.summary: M2AD数据集通过将说明书与视频对齐来评估多模态大语言模型在家具组装任务上的能力，揭示了多图像推理的性能限制和实时技术协助的架构约束。

fr.title: Des Instructions à l'Assistance : un Ensemble de Données Alignant les Manuels d'Instructions avec des Vidéos d'Assemblage pour Évaluer les LLM Multimodaux
fr.summary: L'ensemble de données M2AD évalue les LLM multimodaux sur des tâches d'assemblage de meubles en alignant les manuels d'instructions avec la vidéo, révélant les limitations de performance dans le raisonnement multi-image et les contraintes architecturales pour l'assistance technique en temps réel.

de.title: Von Anweisungen zu Unterstützung: Ein Datensatz zur Ausrichtung von Bedienungsanleitungen mit Montagevideos zur Bewertung multimodaler LLMs
de.summary: Der M2AD-Datensatz bewertet multimodale LLMs bei Möbelmontageaufgaben durch Ausrichtung von Bedienungsanleitungen mit Video und zeigt Leistungsbeschränkungen bei Multi-Bild-Reasoning und architektonische Einschränkungen für echtzeitliche technische Unterstützung.

es.title: De Instrucciones a Asistencia: un Conjunto de Datos Alineando Manuales de Instrucciones con Videos de Montaje para Evaluar LLM Multimodales
es.summary: El conjunto de datos M2AD evalúa LLM multimodales en tareas de montaje de muebles alineando manuales de instrucciones con video, revelando limitaciones de rendimiento en razonamiento multi-imagen y restricciones arquitectónicas para asistencia técnica en tiempo real.

title: AEGIS: An Operational Infrastructure for Post-Market Governance of Adaptive Medical AI Under US and EU Regulations
summary: AEGIS governance framework operationalizes FDA and EU regulatory requirements for adaptive medical AI systems through automated monitoring, retraining, and deployment decisions, with demonstrated application to sepsis prediction and tumor segmentation.
content:
arXiv:2603.22322v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Machine learning systems deployed in medical devices require governance frameworks that ensure safety while enabling continuous improvement. Regulatory bodies including the FDA and European Union have introduced mechanisms such as the Predetermined Change Control Plan (PCCP) and Post-Market Surveillance (PMS) to manage iterative model updates without repeated submissions. This paper presents AI/ML Evaluation and Governance Infrastructure for Safety (AEGIS), a governance framework applicable to any healthcare AI system. AEGIS comprises three modules, i.e., dataset assimilation and retraining, model monitoring, and conditional decision, that operationalize FDA PCCP and EU AI Act Article 43(4) provisions. We implement a four-category deployment decision taxonomy (APPROVE, CONDITIONAL APPROVAL, CLINICAL REVIEW, REJECT) with an independent PMS ALARM signal, enabling detection of the critical state in which no deployable model exists while the released model is simultaneously at risk.
  To illustrate how AEGIS can be instantiated across heterogeneous clinical contexts, we provide two examples: sepsis prediction from electronic health records and brain tumor segmentation from medical imaging. Both cases use identical governance architecture, differing only in configuration. Across 11 simulated iterations on the sepsis example, AEGIS yielded 8 APPROVE, 1 CONDITIONAL APPROVAL, 1 CLINICAL REVIEW, and 1 REJECT decision, exercising all four categories. ALARM signals were co-issued at iterations 8 and 10, including the critical state where no deployable model exists and the released model is simultaneously failing. AEGIS detected drift before observable performance degradation. These results demonstrate that AEGIS translates regulatory change-control concepts into executable governance procedures, supporting safe continuous learning for adaptive medical AI across diverse clinical applications.

zh.title: AEGIS：美国和欧盟法规下自适应医疗AI的市场后治理运营基础设施
zh.summary: AEGIS治理框架通过自动化监测、再训练和部署决策来实现FDA和欧盟对自适应医疗AI系统的监管要求，并在脓毒症预测和肿瘤分割中展示了应用。

fr.title: AEGIS : Une Infrastructure Opérationnelle pour la Gouvernance Post-Marché de l'IA Médicale Adaptative en Vertu des Réglementations Américaines et Européennes
fr.summary: Le cadre de gouvernance AEGIS opérationnalise les exigences réglementaires de la FDA et de l'UE pour les systèmes d'IA médicale adaptative par le biais de la surveillance automatisée, du réentraînement et des décisions de déploiement, avec une application démontrée à la prédiction de la septicémie et à la segmentation des tumeurs.

de.title: AEGIS: Eine operative Infrastruktur für die Governance adaptiver medizinischer KI nach dem Markteintritt gemäß US- und EU-Vorschriften
de.summary: Das AEGIS-Governance-Framework operationalisiert FDA- und EU-Anforderungen für adaptive medizinische KI-Systeme durch automatisierte Überwachung, Umschulung und Bereitstellungsentscheidungen mit nachgewiesener Anwendung auf Sepsisvorhersage und Tumorsegmentierung.

es.title: AEGIS: Una Infraestructura Operativa para la Gobernanza Post-Mercado de IA Médica Adaptativa bajo Regulaciones de EE.UU. y la UE
es.summary: El marco de gobernanza AEGIS operacionaliza los requisitos regulatorios de la FDA y la UE para sistemas de IA médica adaptativa a través de monitoreo automatizado, reentrenamiento y decisiones de implementación, con aplicación demostrada a la predicción de sepsis y segmentación de tumores.

title: A Multi-Task Targeted Learning Framework for Lithium-Ion Battery State-of-Health and Remaining Useful Life
summary: Multi-task framework combining multi-scale CNNs, extended LSTM, and dual-stream attention substantially improves battery state-of-health and remaining useful life predictions, enhancing safety and efficiency of electric vehicle operations.
content:
arXiv:2603.22323v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Accurately predicting the state-of-health (SOH) and remaining useful life (RUL) of lithium-ion batteries is crucial for ensuring the safe and efficient operation of electric vehicles while minimizing associated risks. However, current deep learning methods are limited in their ability to selectively extract features and model time dependencies for these two parameters. Moreover, most existing methods rely on traditional recurrent neural networks, which have inherent shortcomings in long-term time-series modeling. To address these issues, this paper proposes a multi-task targeted learning framework for SOH and RUL prediction, which integrates multiple neural networks, including a multi-scale feature extraction module, an improved extended LSTM, and a dual-stream attention module. First, a feature extraction module with multi-scale CNNs is designed to capture detailed local battery decline patterns. Secondly, an improved extended LSTM network is employed to enhance the model's ability to retain long-term temporal information, thus improving temporal relationship modeling. Building on this, the dual-stream attention module-comprising polarized attention and sparse attention to selectively focus on key information relevant to SOH and RUL, respectively, by assigning higher weights to important features. Finally, a many-to-two mapping is achieved through the dual-task layer. To optimize the model's performance and reduce the need for manual hyperparameter tuning, the Hyperopt optimization algorithm is used. Extensive comparative experiments on battery aging datasets demonstrate that the proposed method reduces the average RMSE for SOH and RUL predictions by 111.3\% and 33.0\%, respectively, compared to traditional and state-of-the-art methods.

zh.title: 锂离子电池健康状态和剩余寿命的多任务目标学习框架
zh.summary: 多任务框架结合多尺度卷积神经网络、扩展长短期记忆和双流注意机制，大幅提高了电池健康状态和剩余寿命预测的准确性，增强了电动汽车运行的安全性和效率。

fr.title: Un cadre d'apprentissage ciblé multi-tâches pour l'état de santé et la durée de vie utile restante des batteries lithium-ion
fr.summary: Le cadre multi-tâches combinant des CNN multi-échelles, un LSTM étendu et une attention bidirectionnelle améliore considérablement les prédictions de l'état de santé et de la durée de vie utile restante des batteries, améliorant la sécurité et l'efficacité des opérations des véhicules électriques.

de.title: Ein Multi-Task-Ziellernen-Framework für den Gesundheitszustand und die verbleibende Lebensdauer von Lithium-Ionen-Batterien
de.summary: Das Multi-Task-Framework kombiniert Multi-Skalen-CNNs, erweitertes LSTM und duale Stream-Aufmerksamkeit und verbessert erheblich die Vorhersagen des Batterie-Gesundheitszustands und der verbleibenden Lebensdauer und erhöht die Sicherheit und Effizienz des Elektrofahrzeugbetriebs.

es.title: Un marco de aprendizaje dirigido multi-tarea para el estado de salud y la vida útil restante de las baterías de iones de litio
es.summary: El marco multi-tarea que combina CNN multiescala, LSTM extendido y atención de doble flujo mejora sustancialmente las predicciones del estado de salud y la vida útil restante de la batería, mejorando la seguridad y eficiencia de las operaciones de vehículos eléctricos.

title: DAQ: Delta-Aware Quantization for Post-Training LLM Weight Compression
summary: DAQ post-training quantization framework preserves post-training knowledge by optimizing for weight delta directional fidelity rather than reconstruction error, recovering style-specific LLM capabilities under FP8 compression.
content:
arXiv:2603.22324v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: We introduce Delta-Aware Quantization (DAQ), a data-free post-training quantization framework that preserves the knowledge acquired during post-training. Standard quantization objectives minimize reconstruction error but are agnostic to the base model, allowing quantization noise to disproportionately corrupt the small-magnitude parameter deltas ($\Delta W$) that encode post-training behavior -- an effect we analyze through the lens of quantization as implicit regularization. DAQ replaces reconstruction-based objectives with two delta-aware metrics -- Sign Preservation Rate and Cosine Similarity -- that directly optimize for directional fidelity of $\Delta W$, requiring only the base and post-trained weight matrices. In a pilot FP8 study, DAQ recovers style-specific capabilities lost under standard quantization while maintaining general performance.

zh.title: DAQ：用于后训练LLM权重压缩的增量感知量化
zh.summary: DAQ后训练量化框架通过优化权重增量方向保真度而非重构误差来保持后训练知识，在FP8压缩下恢复风格特定的大型语言模型能力。

fr.title: DAQ : Quantification sensible aux deltas pour la compression des poids LLM post-entraînement
fr.summary: Le cadre de quantification post-entraînement DAQ préserve les connaissances post-entraînement en optimisant la fidélité directionnelle delta des poids plutôt que l'erreur de reconstruction, récupérant les capacités LLM spécifiques au style sous compression FP8.

de.title: DAQ: Delta-bewusste Quantisierung zur Gewichtskompression von LLM nach dem Training
de.summary: Das DAQ-Post-Training-Quantisierungsframework bewahrt post-Training-Wissen durch Optimierung der Richtungstreue des Gewichtdeltas statt des Rekonstruktionsfehlers und stellt LLM-Fähigkeiten speziell für Stile unter FP8-Kompression wieder her.

es.title: DAQ: Cuantificación consciente del delta para la compresión de pesos de LLM post-entrenamiento
es.summary: El marco de cuantificación post-entrenamiento DAQ conserva el conocimiento post-entrenamiento optimizando la fidelidad direccional del delta de peso en lugar del error de reconstrucción, recuperando capacidades LLM específicas de estilo bajo compresión FP8.

title: Hybrid Associative Memories
summary: HAM layer combines RNNs and self-attention by using attention selectively for information RNNs cannot predict, enabling data-dependent KV cache growth with user-controlled trade-offs between memory cost and performance.
content:
arXiv:2603.22325v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Recurrent neural networks (RNNs) and self-attention are both widely used sequence-mixing layers that maintain an internal memory. However, this memory is constructed using two orthogonal mechanisms: RNNs compress the entire past into a fixed-size state, whereas self-attention's state stores every past time step growing its state (the KV cache) linearly with the sequence length. This results in orthogonal strengths and weaknesses. Self-attention layers excel at retrieving information in the context but have large memory and computational costs, while RNNs are more efficient but degrade over longer contexts and underperform for precise recall tasks. Prior work combining these mechanisms has focused primarily on naively interleaving them to reduce computational cost without regard to their complementary mechanisms. We propose the Hybrid Associative Memory (HAM) layer, which combines self-attention and RNNs while leveraging their individual strengths: the RNN compresses the entire sequence, while attention supplements it *only* with information that is difficult for the RNN to predict, which is hence the most valuable information to explicitly store. HAM layers enable data-dependent growth of the KV cache, which can be precisely controlled by the user with a single, continuous threshold. We find that this fine-grained control of the KV cache growth rate has a smooth trade-off with loss and performance. Empirically, we show that our hybrid architecture offers strong, competitive performance relative to RNNs and Transformers even at substantially lower KV-cache usage.

zh.title: 混合联想记忆
zh.summary: HAM层通过选择性地对循环神经网络无法预测的信息使用注意机制，将递归神经网络和自注意力相结合，实现了数据相关的键值缓存增长，并在内存成本和性能之间实现了用户可控的权衡。

fr.title: Mémoires associatives hybrides
fr.summary: La couche HAM combine les RNN et l'auto-attention en utilisant l'attention sélectivement pour les informations que les RNN ne peuvent pas prédire, permettant une croissance de cache KV dépendante des données avec des compromis contrôlés par l'utilisateur entre le coût mémoire et les performances.

de.title: Hybrid assoziative Speicher
de.summary: Die HAM-Schicht kombiniert RNNs und Self-Attention, indem sie Attention selektiv für Informationen verwendet, die RNNs nicht vorhersagen können, und ermöglicht datenenabhängiges KV-Cache-Wachstum mit benutzergesteuerten Kompromissen zwischen Speicherkosten und Leistung.

es.title: Memorias asociativas híbridas
es.summary: La capa HAM combina RNN y auto-atención usando atención selectivamente para información que los RNN no pueden predecir, permitiendo el crecimiento de caché KV dependiente de datos con compensaciones controladas por el usuario entre el costo de memoria y el rendimiento.

title: A Direct Classification Approach for Reliable Wind Ramp Event Forecasting under Severe Class Imbalance
summary: Novel multivariate time-series classification approach for wind power ramp prediction combines majority-class undersampling and ensemble learning to handle severe class imbalance, achieving 85% accuracy on real-world power grid datasets.
content:
arXiv:2603.22326v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Decision support systems are essential for maintaining grid stability in low-carbon power systems, such as wind power plants, by providing real-time alerts to control room operators regarding potential events, including Wind Power Ramp Events (WPREs). These early warnings enable the timely initiation of more detailed system stability assessments and preventive actions. However, forecasting these events is challenging due to the inherent class imbalance in WPRE datasets, where ramp events are less frequent (typically less than 15\% of observed events) compared to normal conditions. Ignoring this characteristic undermines the performance of conventional machine learning models, which often favor the majority class. This paper introduces a novel methodology for WPRE forecasting as a multivariate time series classification task and proposes a data preprocessing strategy that extracts features from recent power observations and masks unavailable ramp information, making it integrable with traditional real-time ramp identification tools. Particularly, the proposed methodology combines majority-class undersampling and ensemble learning to enhance wind ramp event forecasting under class imbalance. Numerical simulations conducted on a real-world dataset demonstrate the superiority of our approach, achieving over 85% accuracy and 88% weighted F1 score, outperforming benchmark classifiers.

zh.title: 严重类不平衡条件下可靠风速升高事件预测的直接分类方法
zh.summary: 新型多变量时间序列分类方法用于风力发电斜升预测，采用多数类欠采样和集成学习相结合的方式处理严重的类不平衡问题，在真实电网数据集上达到85%的准确率。

fr.title: Une approche de classification directe pour une prévision fiable des rampes de vent sous un déséquilibre de classe grave
fr.summary: Une nouvelle approche de classification de séries chronologiques multivariées pour la prédiction des rampes de puissance éolienne combine le sous-échantillonnage de la classe majoritaire et l'apprentissage d'ensemble pour gérer le déséquilibre de classe grave, atteignant une précision de 85% sur des ensembles de données réels du réseau électrique.

de.title: Ein direkter Klassifizierungsansatz für zuverlässige Windrampen-Event-Vorhersage bei schwerem Klassenungleichgewicht
de.summary: Ein neuartiger multivariater Zeitreihen-Klassifizierungsansatz zur Windleistungs-Rampen-Vorhersage kombiniert Unterabtastung der Mehrheitsklasse und Ensemble-Learning, um schweres Klassenungleichgewicht zu handhaben und erreicht 85% Genauigkeit auf echten Stromnetz-Datensätzen.

es.title: Un enfoque de clasificación directa para una predicción confiable de eventos de rampa eólica bajo desequilibrio de clase grave
es.summary: Un nuevo enfoque de clasificación de series de tiempo multivariadas para la predicción de rampas de potencia eólica combina submuestreo de la clase mayoritaria y aprendizaje conjunto para manejar el desequilibrio de clase grave, logrando una precisión del 85% en conjuntos de datos reales de la red eléctrica.

title: AgentSLR: Automating Systematic Literature Reviews in Epidemiology with Agentic AI
summary: AgentSLR agentic pipeline automates end-to-end systematic literature review workflows for epidemiology, achieving human-level performance while reducing review time from 7 weeks to 20 hours (58x speedup) on WHO-designated pathogens.
content:
arXiv:2603.22327v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Systematic literature reviews are essential for synthesizing scientific evidence but are costly, difficult to scale and time-intensive, creating bottlenecks for evidence-based policy. We study whether large language models can automate the complete systematic review workflow, from article retrieval, article screening, data extraction to report synthesis. Applied to epidemiological reviews of nine WHO-designated priority pathogens and validated against expert-curated ground truth, our open-source agentic pipeline (AgentSLR) achieves performance comparable to human researchers while reducing review time from approximately 7 weeks to 20 hours (a 58x speed-up). Our comparison of five frontier models reveals that performance on SLR is driven less by model size or inference cost than by each model's distinctive capabilities. Through human-in-the-loop validation, we identify key failure modes. Our results demonstrate that agentic AI can substantially accelerate scientific evidence synthesis in specialised domains.

zh.title: AgentSLR: 使用智能体AI自动化流行病学系统文献综述
zh.summary: AgentSLR智能体流程自动化流行病学端到端系统文献综述工作流，在WHO指定的病原体上实现与人类相同的性能，同时将综述时间从7周缩减至20小时（加速58倍）。

fr.title: AgentSLR : Automatisation des revues systématiques de la littérature en épidémiologie avec l'IA agentique
fr.summary: Le pipeline agentique AgentSLR automatise les flux de travail des revues systématiques de la littérature de bout en bout en épidémiologie, atteignant des performances au niveau humain tout en réduisant le temps d'examen de 7 semaines à 20 heures (accélération 58x) sur les agents pathogènes désignés par l'OMS.

de.title: AgentSLR: Automatisierung systematischer Literaturübersichten in der Epidemiologie mit agentischer KI
de.summary: Die AgentSLR-Agentur-Pipeline automatisiert End-to-End-Arbeitsabläufe für systematische Literaturübersichten in der Epidemiologie und erreicht menschliches Leistungsniveau, während die Überprüfungszeit von 7 Wochen auf 20 Stunden (58-fache Beschleunigung) bei von der WHO bezeichneten Krankheitserregern reduziert wird.

es.title: AgentSLR: Automatización de revisiones sistemáticas de la literatura en epidemiología con IA agentiva
es.summary: La tubería agentiva AgentSLR automatiza flujos de trabajo de revisión sistemática de literatura de extremo a extremo en epidemiología, logrando un rendimiento a nivel humano mientras reduce el tiempo de revisión de 7 semanas a 20 horas (aceleración de 58x) en patógenos designados por la OMS.

title: Beyond the Mean: Distribution-Aware Loss Functions for Bimodal Regression
summary: Distribution-aware loss functions using Wasserstein and Cramér distances recover bimodal distributions in regression without mixture model instability, reducing uncertainty estimation error by 45% for trustworthy AI applications.
content:
arXiv:2603.22328v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Despite the strong predictive performance achieved by machine learning models across many application domains, assessing their trustworthiness through reliable estimates of predictive confidence remains a critical challenge. This issue arises in scenarios where the likelihood of error inferred from learned representations follows a bimodal distribution, resulting from the coexistence of confident and ambiguous predictions. Standard regression approaches often struggle to adequately express this predictive uncertainty, as they implicitly assume unimodal Gaussian noise, leading to mean-collapse behavior in such settings. Although Mixture Density Networks (MDNs) can represent different distributions, they suffer from severe optimization instability. We propose a family of distribution-aware loss functions integrating normalized RMSE with Wasserstein and Cram\'er distances. When applied to standard deep regression models, our approach recovers bimodal distributions without the volatility of mixture models. Validated across four experimental stages, our results show that the proposed Wasserstein loss establishes a new Pareto efficiency frontier: matching the stability of standard regression losses like MSE in unimodal tasks while reducing Jensen-Shannon Divergence by 45% on complex bimodal datasets. Our framework strictly dominates MDNs in both fidelity and robustness, offering a reliable tool for aleatoric uncertainty estimation in trustworthy AI systems.
